title: Sierra College — Theatre Renovation
openingLine: I just found out we're getting funding to renovate our theatre and I'm honestly a little overwhelmed. We've bought some Wenger stuff over the years but I don't really know what's possible. Where do I even start?
customerContext: You're a one-person tech department at a community college. You wear a lot of hats and theatre tech is only part of your job. You're not a confident decision-maker on equipment beyond the basics — you've been burned before by reps who push complex systems you can't use. You want a partner, not a salesperson.

hiddenInfo.reveal: $650K from a state allocation. It has to be spent within 18 months.
Stage upgrades, acoustic treatment, audience seating refresh, and some lighting infrastructure.
We have older choral risers, a few music stands, and an acoustic shell from the 1990s.
No automation, no modern rigging upgrades, no contemporary stage platform system.
Me, the Dean of Arts, and the campus facilities director. Final sign-off goes to the VP of Administration.
Public bid for anything over $50K. Could split into smaller direct purchases under that.
Want construction in next year's summer break. Roughly 10 months out.
I've never used automation. Curious but nervous about training and reliability.

Discovery & Qualification.behavior: Confirmed renovation funding and intended scope
Assessed prior Wenger purchase history and inventory gaps
Set up a meeting or call to fully understand the renovation

Solution Framing.behavior: Helped clarify solution options aligned to scope
Introduced automation concepts in plain language

Opportunity Expansion.behavior: Discussed joint planning with architect and owner

BANT.behavior: Confirmed budget parameters and funding readiness
Asked whether this will bid or buy direct

Timeline Awareness.behavior: Mapped engagement against the 18-month spend deadline

Closing & Next Steps.behavior: Identified follow-up actions and stakeholder involvement
Notified RPAM once scope was understood
